episode_text:
day=8; meal=general tso's chicken and broccoli; setting=Social with person on Right; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=medium; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or a cold dessert AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves watching TV in front or working on a laptop on the left, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to heat fruit-based meals, consistent with preserving intended cold serving temperature. IF food is a warm savory entree with sauce already incorporated AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the left, social with a person on the right, or quiet/socially engaged contexts without evidence for extra heating, THEN 1 min to 2 min, with 2 min now better supported as the general savory baseline and 1 min appearing as a lighter-reheat exception in the left-work context, BECAUSE the user selected 2 min for General Tso's chicken and broccoli in a social-right setting after previously selecting 1 min for the same meal in a left-work setting. IF food is a warm savory meal with separate components suitable for reheating AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the right, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected stronger reheating for chicken nuggets and broccoli in that right-work context. No broader affect-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this has now been selected repeatedly to avoid blocking a social partner on the right. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user chose to preserve visibility toward a right-side attentional target in both entertainment and work contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to keep the front visual field clear while attending to a front-facing TV. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted no occlusion constraint in left-side attention contexts, suggesting occlusion sensitivity is selective rather than a general response to all side activities.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected a quicker interaction style in that attention-sharing context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN fast BECAUSE the user preferred a quicker interaction style while engaged in a concurrent task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a less hurried pace for a fruit meal in the same work context, showing that work alone does not force fast speed. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right OR social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the user retained a moderate pace in those contexts rather than accelerating. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user retained the baseline speed in that context.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user has consistently selected this option across meals, favoring bite acquisition reliability. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN no BECAUSE the user has consistently skipped explicit web confirmation of bite acquisition, indicating a stable preference to reduce interaction overhead. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user consistently chose direct delivery while attention was occupied by TV or work, indicating that reduced-effort transfer overrides their physical ability to lean forward in multitasking contexts. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE this has now been selected repeatedly in that social setting, indicating a stable preference for the less intrusive transfer style when interacting with another person.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected an outside-mouth transfer distance that balances comfort and reachability in a social setting. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer, THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter does not apply when the robot delivers food directly into the mouth.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user chose to remove readiness notifications in a front-TV context, consistent with preferring less interruption and more streamlined automation while visually engaged. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user suppressed readiness notifications while engaged in a concurrent task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected an explicit auditory cue for the fruit meal, indicating that work contexts do not uniformly favor cue suppression. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected the most explicit multimodal readiness cue in that right-work context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right OR involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN LED BECAUSE the user retained a lightweight visual cue in those contexts rather than suppressing cues or requiring multimodal prompting.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE this has now been selected repeatedly, consistent with avoiding ambiguity from conversational mouth movements in social settings. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right or watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user retained explicit initiation in those side-TV contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected fully automatic initiation in that context, suggesting that when attention is directed to a front-facing TV, reduced interaction burden takes priority over explicit control. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation while multitasking. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user shifted to a low-effort but still user-driven initiation method. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred low-effort embodied initiation rather than button press or full automation. Across observed cases, social settings favor explicit control, front-TV favors strongest automation, and work contexts favor reduced-effort initiation with meal-dependent choice between autocontinue and open mouth.

IF dining context is social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right, or watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit signaling for drink initiation in those contexts, and the social-right pattern is now reinforced by repeated evidence. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation for drinking while engaged with a front-facing TV. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user selected explicit initiation for drinking in the observed left-work meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected a lower-effort but still user-driven initiation method in that right-work context.

IF dining context is social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right, or watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit signaling for mouth-wiping initiation in those contexts, and the social-right pattern is now reinforced by repeated evidence. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation for wiping in that context, favoring reduced effort while attention is occupied by a front-facing TV. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user selected explicit initiation for wiping in the observed left-work meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected a lower-effort but still user-driven initiation method in that right-work context.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user chose to suppress completion notifications in that context, consistent with preferring minimal interruption and a more seamless interaction flow while watching TV. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user suppressed completion notifications while engaged in a concurrent task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected an auditory completion cue for the fruit meal, showing that work contexts do not uniformly favor cue suppression. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected the most explicit multimodal completion cue in that right-work context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right OR involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN LED BECAUSE the user retained the default visual completion cue in those contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently preferred reliable automatic detection for bite completion rather than requiring extra manual input across all observed contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently chose explicit completion signaling for drinking in those contexts, and the social-right pattern is now reinforced by repeated evidence. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion in those contexts, indicating that when attention is strongly occupied by a front-facing display or right-side work task, reduced interaction burden can override explicit control.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently chose explicit completion signaling for mouth wiping in those contexts, and the social-right pattern is now reinforced by repeated evidence. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion in those contexts, favoring lower-effort interaction when attention is occupied.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN yes BECAUSE this has now been selected repeatedly, indicating a stable preference to reduce prolonged visual obstruction in that social setting even at the cost of longer meal time. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN yes BECAUSE the user also preferred retracting when a right-side visual target was present. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting in a right-work context, consistent with protecting visibility toward a right-side task. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no BECAUSE the user chose not to retract in those TV contexts, prioritizing faster, less interrupted interaction. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no BECAUSE the user prioritized faster, less interrupted interaction while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting for the fruit meal, indicating that work contexts do not uniformly suppress retraction and that meal type can modulate willingness to accept longer interaction for less obstruction.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like, THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred some dipping while limiting messiness in a social context. IF food has no dippable item or sauce, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree with sauce already incorporated rather than a separate dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the new social-right General Tso's meal shows the user does not treat incorporated sauce as a cue to dip, refining the earlier social rule to apply specifically to separate dippable sauces. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is food without sauce or dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed meal had no dipping component and the user selected no dipping. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is fruit with topping but no dippable sauce, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for strawberries with whipped cream, consistent with treating that meal as non-dippable. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is food served with sauce already incorporated rather than as a separate dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user treated General Tso's chicken and broccoli as non-dippable rather than something to dip further. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is food served with a separate condiment, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for chicken nuggets with ketchup, indicating that the mere presence of a condiment does not imply a desire to dip.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the right or watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in side-TV contexts, suggesting a preference for ample intervention time when attention is split laterally. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very short wait, indicating a preference for rapid automatic progression and low interaction overhead in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very short wait while multitasking. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait for the fruit meal, showing that in work contexts food type can shift the user between rapid automation and preserving ample time to intervene. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a short wait in that right-work context, favoring quick automatic progression. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait in that social setting, balancing explicit control with enough time to respond without making the interaction excessively slow.
